name: Coffee Toffee Pie
recipeIngredient: 3/4 cup Finely chopped walnuts or pecans
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (180°C). Lightly butter a 10-inch (25-cm) springform pan. Cut waxed paper to fit the bottom and place it butter-side up in the pan.
2. In a large mixing bowl, stir together flour, chopped nuts, sugar, chopped chocolate, and salt. Stir in butter and vanilla. Add 2 tablespoons water and mix until crumbly. If the mixture is dry, add more water (up to 2 tablespoons total). Press into the lined springform pan, covering the bottom and half of the sides. Bake for 18-20 minutes or until set and edges are just starting to brown. Cool on a rack.
3. For the filling:
- Cut chocolate into coarse pieces.
- Melt chocolate and instant coffee in a small heavy-bottomed saucepan over very low heat, stirring frequently. Remove from heat when small pieces of chocolate remain visible (about 5 minutes). Stir until smooth.
- Alternatively, melt chocolate and instant coffee in a microwave-safe bowl on medium for about 2 minutes, stirring until smooth.
4. In a mixing bowl, beat butter with an electric mixer or wooden spoon until very creamy. Gradually add sugar, beating until light and lemon-colored. Beat in the cooled chocolate mixture until combined. Beat in eggs one at a time, incorporating well after each addition. Pour into the cooled crust and refrigerate for at least 1 hour, up to 2 hours.
5. For the topping:
- Stir instant coffee with cream, vanilla, and liqueur (optional) in a medium bowl. Beat until soft peaks form when beaters are lifted. Gradually add icing sugar. Spoon over the pie and mound over the filling, swirling the surface of the cream. Decorate with chocolate curls or chopped chocolate, if desired.
- Serve chilled immediately or refrigerate for up to 2 hours. Gently remove sides from the pan and serve the pie on the same day it is made.
Nutrients per serving: 6.1 g protein, 40.5g fat, 37.8g carbohydrates, 1.6 mg iron, 59 mg calcium, 524 calories
Source: The Trellis Cafe in Hubbards, Nova Scotia, Canada (printed in Chatelaine, February, 1996)
